titleStmt.title: Letter from Henry Williams to Matthew Nathan, 14 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Henry Williams

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Henry Williams, secretary at the Office of Public Works in Dublin,to sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939), under-secretary for Ireland. Williams informs Nathan that he has been directed by the Commissioners of Public Works to state that they have no observations to offer on the matter of amending the Tralee and Dingle Order of 1896.Sir Matthew Nathan was the under secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/6707

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
2694/16. OFFICE OF PUBLIC WORKS, DUBLIN. 14th April, 1916. Sir, I am directed by the Commissioners of Public
Works to refer to your letter of the 6th instant
(4339) transmitting a draft of an Order proposed
to be made amending the Tralee and Dingle (Committee
of Management) Order 1896, and I am to state that
they have no observations to offer in the matter. I have the honour to be,
Sir,
Your obedient servant, H. Williams
Secretrary. The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle.
